raw_consumer_statement:
Best Buy/Citibank wrongfully claims that I owe over {$8000.00} worth of fraudulent transactions which appeared on my account late last year, XX/XX/2023. I have been defending my innocence ever since then, but Best Buy/Citibank ( BB & Citi ) refuses to re-open my claim since I allegedly do not have any evidence to prove my innocence. 

However, the evidence that BB & Citi has sent to me, which is documentation from various merchants involved in the fraudulent transactions, explicitly name a " XXXX XXXX '' as the individual who made these purchases. " XXXX XXXX '' is NOT my name, nor is it an individual who I know or ever heard of. Furthermore, some of the transactions state that this person made the purchases from places like Florida and XXXX, and I most certainly do NOT live or have ever lived in any of these places. 

Before this arduous incident, my credit score was sitting at a XXXX, and now, because of BB & Citi 's incompetent investigation, it has sunk to about a XXXX. I did NOT make any of these fraudulent transactions that BB & Citi claims I am responsible for.
